Title: Enhancement of vitamin B6 levels in rice expressing Arabidopsis vitamin B6 biosynthesis de novo genes

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Core catalytic subunit of de novo vitamin B6 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the catalytic synthase subunit of the PLP synthase complex that produces pyridoxal 5'-phosphate/vitamin B6 de novo from sugar and triose/ammonia-derived substrates together with PDX2
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Ribose 5-phosphate or ribulose 5-phosphate; glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate or dihydroxyacetone phosphate; ammonia supplied by PDX2
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Pyridoxal 5'-phosphate (PLP), leading to other vitamin B6 vitamers
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the vitamin B6 backbone as part of the de novo synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not formally established; protein accumulation in seeds appears limiting for end-product increase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor for many metabolic reactions; reported antioxidant properties in plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; roots; seed coat/aleurone and embryo of unpolished seeds, with little accumulation in polished endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene transcripts detected in leaves, unpolished seeds, and polished seeds; higher in leaves than seeds under CaMV 35S; not detected in leaves when driven by Glob promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in rice with AtPDX2; RT-qPCR; western blot; yeast bioassay; HPLC vitamer profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases vitamin B6 accumulation in rice leaves and roots and moderately in unpolished seeds, but little/no meaningful increase in polished endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; hydroponics; seed tissue context; comparison of constitutive CaMV 35S and endosperm-specific Glob promoter expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; rice Globulin (Glob) endosperm-specific promoter
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR expression analysis; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some high-vitamin B6 lines showed reduced plant height and significant seed yield penalty mainly due to reduced 100-seed mass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for vitamin B6 biofortification of vegetative tissues and outer seed tissues; likely requires improved seed/endosperm protein accumulation and possibly stacking with factors for precursor supply, transport, or stabilization to enrich polished rice
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein was detectable in leaves but not in seeds despite transcript presence, suggesting post-transcriptional/protein-level limitation in seed tissues; major accumulated forms included PN-Glu and unphosphorylated vitamers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive co-expression of AtPDX1.1 with AtPDX2 in rice markedly increased total vitamin B6, up to 28.3-fold in leaves. 2. In seeds, enhancement was mainly in embryo/outer tissues and not substantially in polished endosperm. 3. AtPDX1.1 transcripts were present in seeds, but protein was only detected in leaves, indicating seed-specific limitation at or after translation.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Glutaminase subunit of vitamin B6 biosynthesis protein PDX2
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Core partner subunit supplying ammonia to the PLP synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes glutamine to generate ammonia used by PDX1 for de novo pyridoxal 5'-phosphate synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies essential nitrogen for direct de novo vitamin B6 synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key partner in pathway; rate limitation not established independently
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor for many metabolic reactions; reported antioxidant properties in plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; roots; seed coat/aleurone and embryo of unpolished seeds, with little accumulation in polished endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene transcripts detected in leaves, unpolished seeds, and polished seeds; higher in leaves than seeds under CaMV 35S; not detected in leaves when driven by Glob promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic co-overexpression in rice with AtPDX1.1; RT-qPCR; western blot; yeast bioassay; HPLC vitamer profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with AtPDX1.1 to large increases in rice vitamin B6 in leaves/roots and smaller increases in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; hydroponics; seed tissue context; comparison of constitutive CaMV 35S and endosperm-specific Glob promoter expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; rice Globulin (Glob) endosperm-specific promoter
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR expression analysis; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In co-expression lines, some reduction in seed yield and 100-seed mass
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of a PDX1/PDX2 module for vitamin B6 biofortification; should be paired with strategies overcoming poor seed protein accumulation for endosperm enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein accumulation in seeds could not be clearly interpreted because of cross-reacting bands in western blots; effect demonstrated only together with AtPDX1.1 in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of AtPDX2 with AtPDX1.1 enabled substantial vitamin B6 enhancement in rice. 2. Transcript accumulation occurred in leaves and seeds, but seed vitamin gain remained limited. 3. Seed-level bottlenecks likely occur downstream of transcription, consistent with poor detectable PDX protein accumulation in seeds.
